 {"id":"bd-qqowd","title":"add mounted-path btrfs read-only EROFS coverage for fsync fdatasync and fsyncdir","description":"Survey finding: btrfs fuse_e2e currently proves fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir success on writable mounts, but the mounted-path read-only rejection contract is not frozen even though ffs-fuse and ffs-core already return EROFS for these sync operations on read-only mounts. Scope: add btrfs read-only mounted-path probes for file sync_all, sync_data, and directory sync_all that assert exact EROFS and unchanged data/dirent state. Acceptance: btrfs FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only sync refusal for fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:20:27.269990402Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:32:36.746576644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:32:36.744719148Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-qrqdk","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] Jbd2Writer emits unchecksummed descriptor/revoke/commit blocks — replay with verify_checksums=true rejects them on checksummed journals","description":"# Review finding (review-only pass on 2026-04-23)

## Location

`crates/ffs-journal/src/lib.rs` `Jbd2Writer::commit_transaction` (lines ~982-1180) and the `Jbd2Writer` struct (~841-851).

## Finding

The JBD2-compatible writer produces three kinds of blocks — descriptor, revoke, and commit — and does not stamp a checksum into any of them:

- Descriptor blocks (L1056-L1095): built with `vec![0; bs]` + header + tags, written verbatim. No tail-checksum at `block[bs-4..bs]`.
- Revoke blocks (L1126-L1153): built with header + entries, written verbatim. No tail-checksum.
- Commit blocks (L1160-L1164): built with just `encode_jbd2_header`; `h_chksum_type`, `h_chksum_size`, and `h_chksum[0]` at offset 16 are all zero bytes. No CRC written.

The replay-side `verify_jbd2_block_checksum` (L282-L321) checks all three types when `sb.has_checksum()` is true:

- Descriptor/revoke: reads `stored = read_be_u32(block, bs-4)`, computes via `checksum_jbd2_tail_zeroed_block(block, seed)`, compares.
- Commit: reads `stored = read_be_u32(block, JBD2_COMMIT_CHKSUM_OFFSET)` (offset 16), computes `!crc32c_append(!seed, &temp)` with the 4 bytes at 16 zeroed, compares.

For writer-produced blocks, `stored == 0` in every case but `computed` is a non-zero CRC of the block bytes, so the comparison returns false and `replay_jbd2_inner` breaks out of the scan (L523).

## Impact

A writer-produced transaction on a checksummed journal (any of `JBD2_FEATURE_COMPAT_CHECKSUM`, `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V2`, `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3`, or `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_ASYNC_COMMIT`) is unreplayable with the default `ReplayOptions { verify_checksums: true }`. Modern `mkfs.ext4` enables `metadata_csum` by default, which sets `JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3` on the journal superblock, so any real-world ext4 image with default features falls into this path.

## Why this is currently latent (but still a bug)

`Jbd2Writer::attach_jbd2_writer` is not called from any production binary today (grep across `crates/ffs-fuse`, `crates/ffs-cli`, `crates/ffs-harness`, `crates/ffs` finds no callers); only tests construct writers. Tests avoid triggering the bug by either running without a JBD2 superblock in the journal, or by post-stamping checksums via the `stamp_commit_checksum` / `stamp_descriptor_or_revoke_checksum` helpers (lines 2591-2608), which exist precisely because the writer does not.

When anyone wires `attach_jbd2_writer` into the `ffs mount --rw` / `commit_transaction_journaled` path (see `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:4312`), a checksummed ext4 image will fail to replay on the next mount, silently losing the transaction's writes.

## Suggested fix

1. Add `has_checksum: bool` and `csum_seed: u32` fields to `Jbd2Writer` alongside `is_64bit`.
2. `Jbd2Writer::open` already reads the journal superblock (L889) — have it also populate these fields via `sb.has_checksum()` and `sb.csum_seed()`.
3. Provide a `Jbd2Writer::with_checksum_config(region, start_seq, has_checksum, csum_seed)` constructor for the `Jbd2Writer::new` callers that need it.
4. In `commit_transaction`, when `has_checksum` is true:
   - After filling a descriptor block but before `dev.write_block`, stamp the tail via `checksum_jbd2_tail_zeroed_block(&desc, self.csum_seed).unwrap()` and write into the last 4 bytes.
   - Same for revoke blocks.
   - For commit blocks, zero `h_chksum[0]` (offset 16), compute `!crc32c::crc32c_append(!seed, &commit)`, write into offset 16.
5. Fix `max_tags_per_descriptor` / `max_revoke_entries` / `blocks_needed` to reserve 4 tail bytes when `has_checksum` is true.

## Acceptance

- New regression test: build a `MemBlockDevice`, stamp a valid JBD2 superblock with `feature_incompat = JBD2_FEATURE_INCOMPAT_CSUM_V3` + a non-zero UUID at region block 0, call `Jbd2Writer::open` (or `with_checksum_config`), commit one transaction with a single write, then call `replay_jbd2_with_options(cx, dev, region, ReplayOptions { verify_checksums: true })` and assert `outcome.stats.replayed_blocks == 1` and the target block was overwritten with the payload.
- Existing non-checksummed tests keep passing.
- `commit_transaction_journaled` in `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` — still has no production caller, but when it does, the journal round-trips through a verify_checksums=true replay.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] — real data-loss bug with a clear trigger (metadata_csum ext4 + Jbd2Writer write path), but unreachable from any shipping binary today. Worth fixing before anyone wires the writer into the mount --rw path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T23:52:19.015585Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:58:44.436959096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:58:44.435067924Z","close_reason":"Jbd2Writer stamps descriptor, revoke, and commit checksums for verified replay","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-qvjnr","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_image smoke fixture uses non-parseable 8-byte ROOT_ITEM payload","description":"# Review finding

`build_btrfs_image()` does not just miss the current default-root `ROOT_ITEM` key; the synthetic `ROOT_ITEM` payload it does emit is only 8 bytes of dummy data. That means the fixture cannot exercise real btrfs root-item parsing and would only open under `BtrfsMountSelection::DefaultRoot` via the fallback path in `resolve_btrfs_mount_selection()`.

## Evidence
- Builder writes a single `ROOT_ITEM` entry with `data_size = 8` and payload `[CA FE BA BE DE AD BE EF]`: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22188-22198`.
- `resolve_btrfs_mount_selection()` treats parse failure on `ROOT_ITEM` specially for `DefaultRoot`: it logs and falls back to the superblock `root_dir_objectid` instead of returning an error: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2418-2428`.
- So even after the separately-filed missing-objectid-5 issue is fixed, this smoke fixture can still pass while never validating a parseable root-item contract.

## Why this is high severity
This leaves the basic btrfs open smoke coverage under-specified: the fixture does not distinguish “default-root selection really parsed a valid root item” from “selection only survived a fallback for malformed synthetic metadata.” That is exactly how workspace-green smoke tests drift away from real mount semantics.

## Repro
Source inspection plus the same workspace gate:
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
When repairing `build_btrfs_image()`, make the synthetic `ROOT_ITEM` payload parseable enough to avoid the default-root fallback path, or split the tests so fallback behavior and parseable-root behavior are covered separately with explicit assertions. Do not accept a green smoke test that only works because `DefaultRoot` fell back after a malformed `ROOT_ITEM` parse.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:40:46.649221236Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:28Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:28Z","close_reason":"Completed: build_btrfs_image now emits a parseable ROOT_ITEM payload and the default-root smoke path no longer relies on fallback","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

None covered by E2E tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T06:21:18.172693136Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:15:30.448715126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:15:30.448689798Z","close_reason":"All four integration scenarios (I-3, I-5, I-6, I-7) already implemented as FUSE E2E tests: fuse_spec_i3_write_and_persist_after_remount, fuse_spec_i5_delete_reclaims_free_counts, fuse_spec_i6_concurrent_writes_no_corruption, fuse_spec_i7_reader_sees_pre_modification_version_during_write","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -3873,6 +3873,9 @@ fn create_impl(engine: &FrankenFsEngine, cx: &Cx, parent: InodeNumber,
   `RWF_NOAPPEND` suppresses an open-file `O_APPEND` offset rewrite, and the
   invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` combination returns `EINVAL` before EOF
   lookup or backend mutation.
+- `RWF_HIPRI` is advisory for FrankenFS V1. When accepted by the host and
+  forwarded through FUSE, it preserves the normal offset write contract and
+  does not imply append, sync, NOWAIT, or unsupported-intent behavior.
 - Non-conflicting write-intent flags that require guarantees FrankenFS does not
   provide in V1 (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE`
   cache-bypass/drop semantics) return `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation and

@@ -286,7 +286,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_all_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `btrfs_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_all_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, leading-hole, and all-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
 | FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for write-intent propagation. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. |
-| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_HIPRI` is treated as a harmless priority/cache hint because bit 0 is also `FUSE_WRITE_CACHE`; coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without sync, NOWAIT, append, or unsupported-intent side effects. `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID` is likewise kept as a metadata write hint despite sharing raw bit 2 with `RWF_SYNC`; unit coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without an accidental full fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file; the Linux-invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` combination rejects with `EINVAL` before EOF lookup or mutation. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, flag-collision hints, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, invalid append/NOAPPEND conflicts, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for `RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_SYNC`, `RWF_APPEND`, `RWF_NOAPPEND`, the invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` pair, and unsupported `RWF_ATOMIC`/`RWF_DONTCACHE` intents, proving sync writes become immediately readable and survive remount, append-mode offset semantics reject or mutate exactly as Linux requires, and unsupported intents return `EOPNOTSUPP` without data mutation when the host permits FUSE and forwards the flags. |
+| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_HIPRI` is treated as a harmless priority/cache hint because bit 0 is also `FUSE_WRITE_CACHE`; coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without sync, NOWAIT, append, or unsupported-intent side effects, and mounted-path `os.pwritev(..., RWF_HIPRI)` coverage now proves accepted calls preserve caller-offset overwrite semantics. `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID` is likewise kept as a metadata write hint despite sharing raw bit 2 with `RWF_SYNC`; unit coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without an accidental full fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file; the Linux-invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` combination rejects with `EINVAL` before EOF lookup or mutation. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, flag-collision hints, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, invalid append/NOAPPEND conflicts, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for `RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_SYNC`, `RWF_HIPRI`, `RWF_APPEND`, `RWF_NOAPPEND`, the invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` pair, and unsupported `RWF_ATOMIC`/`RWF_DONTCACHE` intents, proving sync writes become immediately readable and survive remount, harmless hints preserve normal offset writes, append-mode offset semantics reject or mutate exactly as Linux requires, and unsupported intents return `EOPNOTSUPP` without data mutation when the host permits FUSE and forwards the flags. |
 | FUSE inotify rename + dentry notification lifecycle | `fs/notify/fsnotify.c`, `fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c`, FUSE notify protocol | ✅ | `FrankenFuse` captures a `fuser::Notifier` during blocking, background, and managed mount setup, then issues `FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY` for successfully committed `unlink`, `rmdir`, and both old/new directory entries after `rename`. Plain renames deliberately avoid `FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE`, preserving the kernel/VFS `IN_MOVED_FROM` + `IN_MOVED_TO` cookie-pair contract instead of synthesizing delete/create observations. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now carries mounted-path inotify probes for 100 same-parent renames and a cross-directory rename; both assert non-zero matching cookies and reject `IN_CREATE`, `IN_DELETE`, `IN_Q_OVERFLOW`, and `IN_IGNORED` during plain rename bursts, with the standard CI soft-skip when the current host denies FUSE mounting. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |

@@ -1474,6 +1474,70 @@ print(json.dumps({
 }, sort_keys=True))
 "#;
 
+const PWRITEV2_RWF_HINT_SCRIPT: &str = r#"
+import errno
+import json
+import os
+import sys
+
+root = sys.argv[1]
+flag_name = sys.argv[2]
+file_name = sys.argv[3]
+initial = bytes.fromhex(sys.argv[4])
+payload = bytes.fromhex(sys.argv[5])
+offset = int(sys.argv[6])
+path = os.path.join(root, file_name)
+
+if not hasattr(os, "pwritev"):
+    print(json.dumps({"skipped": "python_os_pwritev_unavailable"}))
+    sys.exit(0)
+
+flag = getattr(os, flag_name, None)
+if flag is None:
+    print(json.dumps({"skipped": "python_os_" + flag_name.lower() + "_unavailable"}))
+    sys.exit(0)
+
+unsupported_errnos = {errno.EINVAL, errno.EOPNOTSUPP}
+if hasattr(errno, "ENOTSUP"):
+    unsupported_errnos.add(errno.ENOTSUP)
+
+with open(path, "wb") as fh:
+    fh.write(initial)
+
+fd = os.open(path, os.O_WRONLY)
+try:
+    try:
+        written = os.pwritev(fd, [payload], offset, flag)
+    except OSError as exc:
+        if exc.errno in unsupported_errnos:
+            with open(path, "rb") as fh:
+                readback = fh.read()
+            print(json.dumps({
+                "skipped": flag_name.lower() + "_not_forwarded_or_unsupported",
+                "errno": exc.errno,
+                "expected_hex": initial.hex(),
+                "readback_hex": readback.hex(),
+            }, sort_keys=True))
+            sys.exit(0)
+        raise
+finally:
+    os.close(fd)
+
+expected = initial[:offset] + payload + initial[offset + len(payload):]
+with open(path, "rb") as fh:
+    readback = fh.read()
+
+print(json.dumps({
+    "expected_hex": expected.hex(),
+    "flag": flag,
+    "offset": offset,
+    "path": path,
+    "payload_hex": payload.hex(),
+    "readback_hex": readback.hex(),
+    "written": written,
+}, sort_keys=True))
+"#;
+
 const PWRITEV2_RWF_APPEND_MODES_SCRIPT: &str = r#"
 import errno
 import json
@@ -1661,6 +1725,34 @@ fn run_pwritev2_rwf_probe(root: &Path, flag_name: &str, file_name: &str, payload
     serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).expect("pwritev2 RWF probe JSON")
 }
 
+fn run_pwritev2_rwf_hint_probe(
+    root: &Path,
+    flag_name: &str,
+    file_name: &str,
+    initial: &[u8],
+    payload: &[u8],
+    offset: u64,
+) -> Value {
+    let output = Command::new("python3")
+        .arg("-c")
+        .arg(PWRITEV2_RWF_HINT_SCRIPT)
+        .arg(root)
+        .arg(flag_name)
+        .arg(file_name)
+        .arg(bytes_to_hex(initial))
+        .arg(bytes_to_hex(payload))
+        .arg(offset.to_string())
+        .output()
+        .expect("run python3 pwritev2 RWF hint probe");
+    assert!(
+        output.status.success(),
+        "python3 pwritev2 {flag_name} hint probe failed for {}: {}",
+        root.display(),
+        String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr)
+    );
+    serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).expect("pwritev2 RWF hint probe JSON")
+}

+#[test]
+fn ext4_fuse_pwritev2_rwf_hipri_preserves_normal_offset_write() {
+    if !fuse_available() {
+        eprintln!("FUSE prerequisites not met, skipping");
+        return;
+    }
+
+    let tmp = TempDir::new().expect("tmpdir");
+    let image = create_test_image_with_size(tmp.path(), 4 * 1024 * 1024);
+    let mnt = tmp.path().join("mnt");
+    fs::create_dir_all(&mnt).expect("create mountpoint");
+    let mount_opts = MountOptions {
+        read_only: false,
+        auto_unmount: false,
+        ..MountOptions::default()
+    };
+    let Some(_session) = try_mount_ffs_rw_with_options(&image, &mnt, &mount_opts) else {
+        return;
+    };
+
+    let report = run_pwritev2_rwf_hint_probe(
+        &mnt,
+        "RWF_HIPRI",
+        "rwf_hipri_hint_probe.bin",
+        b"abcdef",
+        b"hipri",
+        1,
+    );
+    if let Some(skip_reason) = report["skipped"].as_str() {
+        assert_eq!(
+            report["readback_hex"].as_str(),
+            report["expected_hex"].as_str(),
+            "transport-level RWF_HIPRI refusal must not mutate data: {report}"
+        );
+        emit_scenario_result("ext4_rw_pwritev2_rwf_hipri_hint", "SKIP", Some(skip_reason));
+        return;
+    }
+
+    assert_eq!(
+        report["written"].as_i64(),
+        Some(i64::try_from(b"hipri".len()).expect("payload length fits i64")),
+        "RWF_HIPRI should report the normal write length: {report}"
+    );
+    assert_eq!(
+        report["readback_hex"].as_str(),
+        report["expected_hex"].as_str(),
+        "RWF_HIPRI should preserve caller offset and normal write semantics: {report}"
+    );
+
+    emit_scenario_result(
+        "ext4_rw_pwritev2_rwf_hipri_hint",
+        "PASS",
+        Some("rwf_hipri_normal_offset_write"),
+    );
+}
